name: privacy-policy
title: Privacy Policy
main_section_html:
Last updated: June 16, 2026

This Privacy Policy explains how Sapphire Fountains LLC (“Sapphire Fountains”, “we”, “us” or “our”) collects, uses, discloses and safeguards information in connection with our websites, customer portal, field-service and back-office systems, telephony and messaging features, device-management features, and related online services (collectively, the “Services”). By using the Services or providing information to us, you acknowledge the practices described here.

1. Who we are

Sapphire Fountains LLC is a company based in Bountiful, Utah, USA that provides water-feature design, installation and maintenance services and operates the supporting business systems described in this policy. For privacy questions, contact us at info@sapphirefountains.com.

2. Information we collect

2.1 Information you provide

  • Account and contact information — name, business name, email address, telephone and mobile numbers, billing and service-site addresses, and role/title.
  • Customer and project records — service requests, project details, maintenance history, equipment and readings at your site, and sign-offs or approvals you provide (including your name and the date of approval).
  • Communications — the content of emails, messages, voicemails and forms you send us.

2.2 Information collected automatically

  • Device and usage data — IP address, browser type, pages viewed, and similar log data when you use our websites or portal.
  • Cookies and analytics — we use cookies and analytics services (including Google Analytics) to understand and improve usage. See Section 6.

2.3 Precise location information (field staff)

Our field time-tracking application (the “Time Kiosk”) may collect precise geolocation (GPS latitude/longitude and timestamps) from a user’s device while that user is clocked in, for time-and-attendance and job-verification purposes. This collection applies to authorized field personnel, is disclosed to them, and depends on device permissions the user can control. Location records are retained only as long as needed for these purposes and are routinely purged.

2.4 Telephone and call data

When you call us or we call you using our telephony features, we may collect phone numbers, call metadata (time, duration, direction), voicemails, call recordings, and machine-generated transcripts and analyses (such as summaries or sentiment indicators) used to improve service quality and follow-up. Where required by law, calls are recorded only with appropriate notice or consent.

2.5 Text-message (SMS) data

If you opt in to text messaging, we collect your mobile number and the content and metadata of messages exchanged. See the dedicated SMS/Text Messaging terms in Section 5.

2.6 Device-management data

For mobile and computer devices enrolled in our device-management program (which may include company-owned and approved personally-owned “BYOD” devices used for work), we collect device identifiers (such as serial number, IMEI and MAC address), operating-system and compliance status, and the ability to perform administrative actions (such as locking, locating, restarting, deploying software/patches, or remotely wiping the device or its managed work data). On personally-owned devices we seek to limit management to work-related data and functions; however, certain actions (for example, a full device wipe) may affect personal data, as described in our End-User License Agreement.

2.7 Financial and transaction data

We process invoices, payments, and related accounting records, which we synchronize with our accounting system (Intuit QuickBooks Online).

2.8 Information from third parties

We may receive information from integrated third-party services you or we connect (for example, Google Workspace, Intuit QuickBooks Online, and our telephony provider), consistent with those services’ terms and your authorizations.

3. How we use information

  • Provide, operate, maintain and secure the Services;
  • Schedule, perform, document and bill for field-service and project work;
  • Track time and attendance and verify on-site work;
  • Communicate with you by phone, email and text about service, accounts and support;
  • Manage and secure enrolled devices;
  • Maintain accounting and financial records;
  • Generate drafts, summaries and insights using automated and AI-assisted tools (see Section 9);
  • Analyze and improve the Services; and
  • Comply with legal obligations and enforce our agreements.

4. Legal bases for processing (EEA/UK)

Where the GDPR or UK GDPR applies, we process personal data on the bases of: performance of a contract; our legitimate interests in operating, securing and improving the Services and our business; your consent (for example, for precise location, certain cookies, or marketing texts), which you may withdraw; and compliance with legal obligations.

5. SMS/Text messaging

If you provide your mobile number and opt in, you may receive text messages from us such as service and appointment notifications, maintenance and account updates, and support communications. Message frequency varies. Message and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to any message to opt out at any time, and reply HELP for help. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages.

We do not sell or share mobile phone numbers, opt-in/consent information, or text-messaging data with third parties or affiliates for their own marketing or promotional purposes. Mobile information is shared only with service providers that help us deliver the messaging service (such as our messaging platform), and only as needed to provide the Services.

6. Cookies and analytics

We and our analytics providers use cookies and similar technologies to operate our sites and measure usage. You can control cookies through your browser settings. You can opt out of Google Analytics using Google’s opt-out browser add-on. Where required, we request consent before setting non-essential cookies.

7. How we share information

We do not sell your personal information. We share information only as follows:

  • Service providers / sub-processors that process data on our behalf under contract, including: Google (Google Workspace — Drive and Calendar — and Google Cloud / Vertex AI), Intuit (QuickBooks Online), our telephony and messaging provider, our device-management providers, and our AI assistant providers (see Sections 8–9);
  • Professional advisors and authorities when required to comply with law, legal process, or to protect rights, safety and security;
  • Business transfers in connection with a merger, acquisition or sale of assets, subject to this policy; and
  • With your direction or consent.

8. Google API Services — Limited Use

Some features access Google user data through Google APIs (for example, Google Drive and Google Calendar). Sapphire Fountains LLC’s use and transfer of information received from Google APIs to any other app will adhere to the Google API Services User Data Policy, including the Limited Use requirements. Specifically, we use Google user data only to provide or improve user-facing features that are prominent in the Services; we do not use it for advertising; we do not sell it; we do not transfer it to others except as necessary to provide or improve those features, to comply with applicable law, or as part of a merger/acquisition/sale of assets with notice to users; and we do not allow humans to read it unless we have your consent for specific data, it is necessary for security or to comply with law, or the data has been aggregated and anonymized.

9. Automated and AI-assisted features

The Services use automated and AI/large-language-model tools — including Google Cloud Vertex AI (Gemini) and Anthropic (Claude) — to draft communications, generate briefings and summaries, and analyze calls and business data. These providers process the data on our behalf as service providers and, under their applicable enterprise terms, do not use it to train their foundation models. AI-generated output may be inaccurate or incomplete and is reviewed by our personnel before it is relied upon; it does not constitute professional advice.

10. International data transfers

We are based in the United States and may process and store information in the U.S. and other countries. Where we transfer personal data out of the EEA, UK or Switzerland, we rely on appropriate safeguards such as the European Commission’s Standard Contractual Clauses.

11. Data retention

We retain personal information for as long as needed to provide the Services and for legitimate business and legal purposes (for example, to maintain service, accounting and tax records). Certain data is kept only briefly — for example, precise location logs are routinely purged. When information is no longer needed, we delete or de-identify it.

12. Security

We use administrative, technical and physical safeguards designed to protect personal information, including encryption of credentials and access controls. No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

13. Your rights and choices

Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or port your personal information, to object to or restrict certain processing, and to withdraw consent. To exercise any right, contact us at info@sapphirefountains.com. We will verify your request and respond as required by law. You will not be discriminated against for exercising your rights.

13.1 California (CCPA/CPRA)

If you are a California resident, you have the right to know the categories and specific pieces of personal information we collect, use and disclose; to request deletion or correction of your personal information; and to opt out of the “sale” or “sharing” of personal information and to limit use of sensitive personal information. We do not sell or share personal information as those terms are defined under California law, and we do not use sensitive personal information for purposes that require an opt-out. In the preceding 12 months we may have collected the categories of personal information described in Section 2 (identifiers; customer records and commercial information; internet/usage activity; precise geolocation for field staff; audio/electronic information such as call recordings; professional/employment information; and financial information), for the purposes in Section 3, and disclosed them to the service providers in Section 7. You may submit a request by emailing info@sapphirefountains.com, and you may use an authorized agent. California’s “Shine the Light” law: we do not share personal information with third parties for their direct marketing.

13.2 EEA, UK and Switzerland (GDPR)

For these regions, Sapphire Fountains LLC is the controller of personal data processed through the Services. You have the rights described above and the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority. Our legal bases are described in Section 4, and our transfer safeguards in Section 10. To exercise your rights or reach our privacy contact, email info@sapphirefountains.com.

14. Children’s privacy

The Services are intended for businesses and adults and are not directed to children. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 (or under 16 in the EEA/UK). If you believe a child has provided us personal information, contact us and we will delete it.

15. Third-party links and services

The Services may link to or integrate third-party services that have their own privacy practices. We are not responsible for those third parties, and we encourage you to review their policies.

16. Changes to this Privacy Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will revise the “Last updated” date above and, where appropriate, provide additional notice. Your continued use of the Services after an update means you accept the revised policy.

17. Contact us

Questions, requests or complaints about this Privacy Policy or our data practices may be directed to:

Sapphire Fountains LLC
Email: info@sapphirefountains.com
Bountiful, Utah, USA
meta_description: How Sapphire Fountains LLC collects, uses, shares and protects personal information across its websites, portal and services.

name: eula
title: End-User License Agreement
main_section_html:
Last updated: June 16, 2026

This End-User License Agreement (“Agreement”) is a binding agreement between Sapphire Fountains LLC (“Sapphire Fountains”, “we”, “us” or “our”) and you, the person or entity accessing or using our websites, customer portal, field-service and back-office systems, telephony and messaging features, device-management features, and related online services (collectively, the “Services”). It applies to all Authorized Users, including (a) customers and their representatives who access the customer portal or other online services we provide, and (b) our employees and contractors who use our internal systems. By accessing or using the Services, you agree to this Agreement and to our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the Services.

1. Definitions

  • “Authorized User” means a person we permit to access the Services, as described above.
  • “Content” means data, text, files and other materials made available through the Services.
  • “Enrolled Device” means a mobile or computer device enrolled in our device-management program, whether company-owned or an approved personally-owned (“BYOD”) device used for work.

2. License and right to use

Subject to this Agreement, we grant you a limited, revocable, non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable right to access and use the Services solely for their intended business purpose: for customers, to interact with us and view your own records; for employees and contractors, to perform work for Sapphire Fountains in accordance with your engagement and our policies. We reserve all rights not expressly granted.

3. Accounts and security

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activity under your account. You must provide accurate information, keep it current, and promptly notify us at info@sapphirefountains.com of any unauthorized use or suspected security incident. We may suspend access to protect the Services.

4. Acceptable use

You agree not to: (a) use the Services unlawfully or in violation of this Agreement; (b) access data or accounts you are not authorized to access; (c) interfere with, disrupt, or attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Services or related systems; (d) copy, scrape, reverse engineer, decompile or create derivative works from the Services except as permitted by law; (e) upload malicious code or infringing, defamatory or unlawful Content; or (f) use the Services to send unsolicited communications. You will comply with all applicable laws, including those governing privacy, communications and recording of calls.

5. Customer data and confidentiality

As between you and us, customers retain their rights in the Content they submit, and grant us the rights necessary to host, process and use that Content to provide the Services and as described in the Privacy Policy. Authorized Users must keep confidential any non-public information accessed through the Services and use it only for its intended purpose.

6. Calls and text messages

By providing a telephone or mobile number, you consent to receive service-related calls and text messages from us at that number, and you acknowledge that calls may be recorded where permitted by law with appropriate notice. Message frequency varies, and message and data rates may apply. You may opt out of texts by replying STOP, and obtain help by replying HELP. See the SMS terms in our Privacy Policy.

7. AI-assisted features

The Services include automated and AI-assisted features (for example, drafting messages, generating briefings and summaries, and analyzing calls). These features are provided “as is,” may produce inaccurate or incomplete results, and are not a substitute for professional judgment. You are responsible for reviewing AI-generated output before relying on or sending it.

8. Device management

If you use an Enrolled Device, you consent to our management of that device, which may include enforcing security settings and performing administrative actions such as locking, locating, restarting, deploying software or patches, and remotely wiping the device or its managed work data. Some actions, including a full device wipe, are irreversible and may delete data, including personal data on a personally-owned device. We seek to limit management of personally-owned devices to work-related data and functions, but you are responsible for backing up personal data. Device management is further described in the Privacy Policy.

9. Third-party services

The Services interoperate with third-party services (for example, Google, Intuit QuickBooks Online, our telephony/messaging provider, and our device-management providers). Your use of those services is governed by their terms, and we are not responsible for third-party services or their acts or omissions.

10. Intellectual property

The Services, including all software, interfaces, and related materials, and all intellectual-property rights in them, are and remain owned by Sapphire Fountains or its licensors. If you provide feedback or suggestions, you grant us a perpetual, royalty-free license to use them without restriction.

11. Privacy

Our collection and use of information is described in the Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into this Agreement by reference.

12. Disclaimer of warranties

THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED “AS IS” AND “AS AVAILABLE,” WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. WE DO NOT WARRANT THAT THE SERVICES WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE OR SECURE.

13. Limitation of liability

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, SAPPHIRE FOUNTAINS LLC AND ITS SUPPLIERS WILL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOST PROFITS, REVENUE, DATA OR GOODWILL, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THE SERVICES OR THIS AGREEMENT. OUR TOTAL LIABILITY FOR ALL CLAIMS RELATING TO THE SERVICES WILL NOT EXCEED ONE HUNDRED U.S. DOLLARS (US$100) OR, IF YOU ARE A PAYING CUSTOMER, THE AMOUNTS YOU PAID US FOR THE SERVICES IN THE THREE MONTHS BEFORE THE CLAIM, WHICHEVER IS GREATER. SOME JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW CERTAIN LIMITATIONS, SO SOME OF THESE MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU.

14. Indemnification

You will indemnify and hold harmless Sapphire Fountains from claims, damages and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of your misuse of the Services or violation of this Agreement or applicable law, to the extent permitted by law.

15. Term and termination

This Agreement applies while you access the Services. We may suspend or terminate your access at any time, including for violation of this Agreement or to protect the Services. Provisions that by their nature should survive (including Sections 5, 10, 12–14 and 16) will survive termination.

16. Governing law and disputes

This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Utah, USA, without regard to conflict-of-laws rules. The exclusive venue for disputes will be the state and federal courts located in Utah, and you consent to their jurisdiction, except that either party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.

17. Changes to this Agreement

We may update this Agreement from time to time. We will revise the “Last updated” date above, and your continued use of the Services after an update constitutes acceptance of the revised Agreement.

18. Contact

Sapphire Fountains LLC
Email: info@sapphirefountains.com
Bountiful, Utah, USA
meta_description: The terms governing use of Sapphire Fountains LLC online services, customer portal and internal systems.
